name: multi-agent-module
description: Example workflow showing a single DAG node internally coordinated by multiple real Pi sub-agents: manager, developer, tester, final manager.

nodes.title: Module Build
nodes.goal: Implement the target module using managed collaboration between manager, developer, and tester sub-agents.

protocol.rule: Only the manager decides which agent acts next. Agents do not broadcast requests or review unassigned work.

responsibilities: Read the spec and create a scoped implementation plan
Implement only the manager-assigned module scope
Send a directed message back to manager
Review only when manager requests it
Check acceptance criteria, edge cases, and regression risks
Write shared/test-report.md and a directed message to manager

phases.goal: Create a concise implementation plan and directed task for developer.
phases.prompt: Read the spec. Write shared/plan.md with scope, approach, files likely involved, risks, and acceptance criteria. Then write messages/manager-to-developer.jsonl with a developer task. Do not ask tester to act yet.

phases.goal: Implement the manager-assigned module work and report what changed.
phases.prompt: Read shared/plan.md and messages/manager-to-developer.jsonl. Implement the assigned work if the spec is concrete enough. Write shared/implementation-report.md with changed files, decisions, and remaining risks. Write messages/developer-to-manager.jsonl. Do not ask tester directly.

phases.goal: Review the developer output against acceptance criteria and regression risks.
phases.prompt: Read the spec, shared/plan.md, and shared/implementation-report.md. Review only the assigned scope. Write shared/test-report.md with pass/fail observations, missing tests, and recommended fixes. Write messages/tester-to-manager.jsonl. Do not expand into unrelated review dimensions.

phases.goal: Decide final node completion and write parent node outputs.
phases.prompt: Read all shared artifacts and messages. Write report.md as the final module-build report. Write result.json with status completed if the node goal was addressed, needs-revision if implementation is incomplete but actionable, or failed only for execution failure. Include outputs ["report.md", "result.json"].

criteria: Developer addressed the assigned module scope or clearly explained why it could not be implemented
Tester reviewed only the assigned scope
Final report summarizes plan, implementation, test review, and remaining risks
